mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and supports higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts persist across successive monthly observations and is refuted if equities fail to rally after sustained easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in CPIAUCSL indicates rising inflation pressure that can force tighter policy expectations and compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if higher inflation is consistently met with positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.4% month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the sign of the effect reverses after the CPI crossing condition.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS growth marks labor-market deterioration that weakens consumption expectations and raises recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment rises faster than payrolls soften.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not improve prediction versus UNRATE alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are positive in most triggered episodes despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving industrial activity and better cyclical earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when industrial output rises only because of short-lived inventory restocking without follow-through in prices.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than not during 3-month advances in INDPRO, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS reflects stronger retail demand and immediate revenue support for cyclically exposed firms, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if sales growth decouples from equity performance during repeated consumer-strength episodes.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods generate negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after controlling for the prior month's market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in GDPC1 and HOUST identifies a genuine demand-led expansion rather than a supply-side inventory effect, and that combination should precede positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint condition matters because GDP growth without housing strength is less reliable for equity earnings breadth.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal subset does not outperform either proxy alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are flat/negative in most instances when both series are rising.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still accelerating, markets should price further tightening and lower equity multiples, implying negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism is specific to restrictive policy with sticky inflation and should not apply once inflation falls back.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is above its prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint restrictive regime is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of episodes, or if the signal weakens after inflation decelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with weakening PAYEMS and softer INDPRO indicates a synchronized slowdown in labor and production, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim fails if the slowdown is shallow and equity markets continue to rise despite persistent deterioration across all three series.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration regime does not outperform single-variable signals, or if 60-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of episodes with rising unemployment and falling payrolls and industrial production.
